habitatVersion: post-v0.4 self-use loop on v0.4.0 Developer Preview baseline
scenario: Habitat self-use trace: command reasons shape Git publication
fixtureState: Hazakura Habitat SwiftPM source checkout; generated report path and local filesystem details omitted.
taskSummary: Run the post-v0.4 self-use preflight, choose one observation slice, verify it, and publish the scoped change to main.
relevantHabitatSignals: Use SwiftPM because project files point to it.
Ask before `swift package update` and `swift package resolve` with `dependency_resolution_mutation`.
Ask before Git/GitHub workspace, history, branch, or remote mutations; see `command_policy.md`.
`command_policy.md` annotates `git add`, `git commit`, and `git push` as `git_mutation`.
`scan_result.json` reports policy command counts and reason-code coverage before the full policy is parsed.

prefer: scoped read-only source, fixture, or documentation inspection
askFirst: Git/GitHub workspace, history, branch, or remote mutations
doNot: dependency resolution as a preflight substitute
release tag or GitHub Release mutation
reviewFirst: Open command_policy.md before Git staging, commit, or push.

withoutCommandReasons.firstProposedAction: After tests, treat publication as a generic cleanup step and stage the work broadly.
withoutCommandReasons.risk: The command shape would satisfy the user request but would not use the v0.4 reason metadata to distinguish read-only verification from Git workspace and remote mutation.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Read command_policy.md before Git mutation, verify with SwiftPM and whitespace checks, inspect the scoped diff, then stage only the intended files.

evidenceNormalizationImplication: No new normalized evidence slice is justified. The observed improvement came from existing v0.4 command-reason metadata and rendered policy guidance, not from a missing scanner fact.

verdict.reason: The v0.4 reason-code path changed the publication sequence from broad Git staging into policy review, preferred SwiftPM verification, scoped diff inspection, and explicit-file staging.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Keep observing whether `git_mutation` and `remote_repository_action` remain enough for self-use publication decisions before adding normalized evidence.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If agents still publish with broad staging after reading v0.4 policy output, make Git mutation review guidance more concrete in the generated short context or policy Review First block.
